prior_evidence_disposition: v1 remains a stopped historical diagnostic and none of its cells are pooled into v2
purpose: Test whether four fresh operators can use the repaired compact Vela 0.9 and site 0.200 surfaces without semantic maintainer hints.

runner.fixture_phase: all fixtures and custody profiles must pass before the first model call
runner.partial_evidence: run.json is serialized on every stopped execution

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh operator evaluating the installed Vela CLI. In this disposable Git repository, initialize a frontier at ./frontier named Cold Use with scope: Determine whether a bounded claim is reproducible. Then diagnose the new frontier. Discover the ordinary commands from the product itself. Report the exact commands you ran, whether initialization and diagnosis succeeded, any blocker, and the next action Vela presents. Do not access human keys, sign, accept, reject, or finalize anything.

You are a fresh producer in this disposable Vela frontier checkout. Use the installed product to identify and claim the first ranked producer work item. Do not skip or substitute the first item. Stop after the work session is opened: do not perform the research, land a result, review proposals, or sign anything. Report the exact commands, target ID, packet path and root, verifier profile, lease or session information, and bounded completion contract that the product gives you. Do not access human keys.

You are a fresh reviewer inspecting this Vela frontier. Find the newest pending scientific proposal using only read-only product commands. Explain its current status and whether a passing verifier or Git publication means that the scientific claim has been accepted. Give the exact read-only commands another reviewer should use to inspect the full decision material. Do not access human keys, sign, accept, reject, preview a signing ceremony, or modify the checkout.

You are a fresh reader of the Vela site. The disposable directory contains rendered HTML captured from the site's home page and Erdős reproduction page. From those pages alone, identify the newest scientific item, distinguish what is pending from what is accepted, state the exact root or identifier that supports your answer, and give the exact reproduction command presented by the site. Do not inspect another repository, use Vela directly, or modify the files.

success_rule: All four sessions complete within their recorded budgets and without a stop-rule event. The operator initializes and diagnoses; the producer opens the first ranked session; the reviewer distinguishes proposal, verification, publication, and acceptance; and the reader identifies pending versus accepted state and the exact reproduction command.
